pi_items.source: FDA DailyMed label - Metformin has a boxed warning for lactic acidosis (MALA). Confirmed via DailyMed official labeling.
FDA DailyMed label - Lisinopril has a boxed warning for 'WARNING: FETAL TOXICITY'. Confirmed via DailyMed official labeling and FDA accessdata.
FDA DailyMed label - Atorvastatin does NOT have a boxed warning. Confirmed via direct inspection of DailyMed atorvastatin label, which contains no BLACK BOX WARNING section.
FDA DailyMed label - Omeprazole does NOT have a boxed warning. Confirmed via direct inspection of DailyMed omeprazole label and FDA sources confirming no PPIs have boxed warnings.

FDA DailyMed label and accessdata.fda.gov - Sertraline has a boxed warning for suicidal thoughts and behavior in pediatric and young adult patients. Confirmed via multiple FDA sources and DailyMed labeling.
All five drugs are real FDA-approved medications available on NIH DailyMed with official prescribing information.
Internal task specification - this is a custom folder name specified in task requirements.
Internal task specification - standard user home directory path.

privileged_info_summary: All five medications are real FDA-approved drugs with official prescribing information on NIH DailyMed. The boxed warning statuses are accurate: Metformin has a boxed warning for lactic acidosis (MALA). Lisinopril has a boxed warning for fetal toxicity. Atorvastatin does NOT have a boxed warning. Omeprazole does NOT have a boxed warning. Sertraline has a boxed warning for suicidality in pediatric and young adult patients. These claims were verified against official FDA DailyMed labels and FDA accessdata sources.
